commands.repair_reason: No automated source repair is indicated; maintainers need to resolve the conflict/status state and decide whether to merge the scoped non-trending fix while leaving trending for follow-up.; Cleared: The diff only changes cursor decoding, regression tests, and changelog text; it adds no dependency, workflow, secret, install, or code-execution surface. commands.review_summary:
The PR accepts full self-describing public skill-list pagination cursors with Convex `getPage()` tie-breaker fields, updates cursor regression tests, and adds an Unreleased changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main emits full `getPage()` index keys as `nextCursor` and then rejects keys longer than the selected sort field count when the cursor is replayed, matching the linked curl report.
commands.review_followup: No automated source repair is indicated; maintainers need to resolve the conflict/status state and decide whether to merge the scoped non-trending fix while leaving trending for follow-up.

target.title: fix(api): accept full skill list pagination cursors
target.body:
## Summary

Accepts the full public skill-list pagination cursor shape emitted by `getPage()` for non-trending public API sorts.

## What changed

- Self-describing public list cursors may include the full helper index key, including Convex tie-breaker fields.
- Legacy raw-array cursor validation remains strict.
- Pagination tests now mock realistic `getPage()` keys and cover first-page to second-page API pagination.

## Public behavior

The public cursor contract is unchanged: clients send the cursor returned by the previous response. The server now accepts the full cursor shape it already emitted instead of treating it as stale or restarting pagination.

## Behavior proof

Live HTTP API proof with the full `nextCursor` emitted by page one replayed into page two:

```text
$ curl -fsS "http://127.0.0.1:3213/api/v1/skills?limit=3&sort=createdAt"
first.status=200
first.slugs=local-agentic-risk-demo,blossom-jobs
first.nextCursor.length=125

$ curl -fsS --get "http://127.0.0.1:3213/api/v1/skills" \
  --data-urlencode "limit=3" \
  --data-urlencode "sort=createdAt" \
  --data-urlencode "cursor=$FIRST_NEXT_CURSOR"
second.status=200
second.slugs=aidex,ministry-weekly,maverick-hubspot-mcp
second.nextCursor.present=yes
```

This proof exercises the real public HTTP handler. The second request uses the opaque cursor emitted by the first response and returns the next page instead of rejecting or restarting pagination.

Focused regression suite:

```text
$ bun run test convex/skills.publicListCursor.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       5 passed (5)
```

## Validation

```text
$ bun run ci:unit
Test Files  204 passed (204)
Tests       1986 passed (1986)
Statements  85.98%
Branches    75.17%
Functions   86.37%
Lines        89.48%
```

```text
$ bun run ci:types-build
tsc, package typechecks, clawhub-mod typecheck, Vite build, and Nitro build passed.
```

Current GitHub CI for this head has `unit`, `packages`, `types-build`, `e2e-http`, and `playwright-smoke` passing, with `playwright-local-auth` still running at the time of this update. The `static` job currently stops at `bun audit` on the existing transitive `ws` advisory `GHSA-58qx-3vcg-4xpx`.
target.author: vyctorbrzezowski
